mnemonics.title: RAT Properties of Partial Order
mnemonics.content: RAT = Reflexive + Antisymmetric + Transitive. Remember: 'RATs Are Tough' - R-eflexive, A-ntisymmetric, T-ransitive

mnemonics.title: Elements in Hasse Diagram
mnemonics.content: BOTTOM to TOP: Minimal elements at bottom, maximal at top. Draw from least to greatest (bottom-up).

mnemonics.title: Join and Meet
mnemonics.content: Join = UP (Least Upper Bound) = SUPREMUM. Meet = DOWN (Greatest Lower Bound) = INFIMUM. J-U-M-P up for Join, M-I-N down for Meet.

acronyms.usage: Used in lattice theory, same as supremum, corresponds to JOIN (∨)
Used in lattice theory, same as infimum, corresponds to MEET (∧)
A set with a partial order relation defined on it
Hasse diagram is always a DAG - no cycles allowed in partial orders

visualTips.visualization: Start with minimal elements at bottom, draw edges upward only to covering elements. Remove transitive edges - if a<b<c, only draw a to b and b to c, not a to c.
Look from TOP down - any element with no element above it is maximal. In diagram, these are topmost vertices with no outgoing edges going up.
Chain = straight vertical line (comparable). Antichain = horizontal spread (incomparable). Draw chains as towers, antichains as parallel elements.
Think of lattice as a building: bottom is foundation (least), top is roof (greatest). Join is climbing up to common ceiling, Meet is descending to common floor.

analogies.analogy: Think of a family tree where not everyone is related - some cousins are incomparable (neither is ancestor of other). Partial because not all pairs have a relationship.
Like a subway map - shows only direct connections (covering relations), not all possible routes (transitive paths). Simplifies complexity.
Like a building's floors - any two people can find a floor they both can reach (one going up to common ceiling, one going down to common floor).
Maximal is like 'tallest in the room' - no one taller than them. Greatest is like 'boss of everyone' - everyone reports to them. All greatest are maximal, but not vice versa.
